stem: The homogeneous equation \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has characteristic roots \( [[r1]] \) and \( [[r2]] \). For the nonhomogeneous equation with forcing term \( [[A]]e^{[[r]]x} \), undetermined coefficients requires the trial particular solution \( y_p = x^{s}\left([[A]]e^{[[r]]x}\right) \), where \( s \) is the smallest nonnegative integer such that no term of \( y_p \) already solves the homogeneous equation (i.e. \( s \) is the multiplicity of \( [[r]] \) as a root of the characteristic equation). Find \( s \).
solution: \( s \) equals the number of times \( [[r]] \) matches a characteristic root. The roots are \( [[r1]] \) and \( [[r2]] \), so \( s = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This assumes there is always exactly one match, regardless of whether the forcing rate actually equals a root.
This assumes a double match, which can only happen with a REPEATED characteristic root.
For a 2nd-order equation, \( s \) can never exceed 2 (there are only two characteristic roots).
This reports the forcing rate itself instead of counting how many roots it matches.
This reports a characteristic root itself instead of counting matches.
This reports the other characteristic root itself instead of counting matches.

stem: The homogeneous equation \( y'' + ([[b]])y' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has a REPEATED characteristic root \( r=[[r0]] \). For the nonhomogeneous equation with forcing term \( [[A]]e^{[[r]]x} \), undetermined coefficients requires the trial particular solution \( y_p = x^{s}\left([[A]]e^{[[r]]x}\right) \), where \( s \) is the multiplicity of \( [[r]] \) as a root of the characteristic equation. Find \( s \).
solution: Since the root \( [[r0]] \) has multiplicity 2, \( s \) is either 0 (no match) or 2 (a match), never 1. Here \( s = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the multiplicity used for a SIMPLE (non-repeated) matching root, not a repeated one.
For a 2nd-order equation, \( s \) can never exceed 2.
For a 2nd-order equation, \( s \) can never exceed 2.
For a 2nd-order equation, \( s \) can never exceed 2.
For a 2nd-order equation, \( s \) can never exceed 2.
This adds the forcing rate and the repeated root together instead of comparing them.

stem: The homogeneous equation \( y'' + ([[c]])y = 0 \) has purely imaginary characteristic roots \( \pm [[w]]i \). For the nonhomogeneous equation with forcing term \( [[A]]\cos([[k]]x) \), undetermined coefficients requires the trial particular solution \( y_p = x^{s}\left(A\cos([[k]]x)+B\sin([[k]]x)\right) \), where \( s \) is the smallest nonnegative integer such that no term of \( y_p \) already solves the homogeneous equation (this happens exactly when the forcing frequency \( [[k]] \) equals \( [[w]] \)). Find \( s \).
solution: Since the roots \( \pm[[w]]i \) are simple (not repeated), \( s \) can only be 0 or 1. It is 1 exactly when the forcing frequency \( [[k]] \) matches \( [[w]] \); here \( s = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: Since the roots \( \pm[[w]]i \) are simple, \( s \) can never reach 2 (that requires a REPEATED matching root), let alone this larger value.
This value is far too large; \( s \) is always 0 or 1 for a simple pair of roots.
This value is far too large; \( s \) is always 0 or 1 for a simple pair of roots.
This value is far too large; \( s \) is always 0 or 1 for a simple pair of roots.
This adds the forcing frequency and natural frequency together instead of comparing them.
